name: Credit Coalition
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency in Houston offering free financial and homebuyer education classes, pre-purchase counseling, and financial coaching to low and moderate-income consumers.
description_long:
Credit Coalition is a Houston-based non-profit organization dedicated to empowering consumers through financial literacy and homebuyer education. Founded to serve all community segments—especially low and moderate-income households—the organization provides free counseling and educational services to help people make informed financial and housing decisions. Their mission centers on helping consumers enter or re-enter the credit mainstream with knowledge, resources, and ongoing support.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of free services including group classes ("Fundamentals of Good Credit" and "Life As A Homeowner"), reverse mortgage counseling, one-on-one credit counseling, financial coaching, pre-purchase counseling, and community financial education. All classes are offered online via Zoom and available in both English and Spanish. The "Fundamentals of Good Credit" certificate fulfills homebuyer education requirements for TSAHC, TDHCA, and several Texas cities (Houston, Baytown, and others). They also provide curated resources on credit reports, fair housing issues, down payment assistance programs, foreclosure prevention, and connections to IDA (Individual Development Account) programs.

Credit Coalition distinguishes itself through its certification status for homebuyer education requirements, multi-language offerings, and deep integration with local housing assistance programs including Habitat for Humanity partnerships. The organization maintains an accessible phone line (713-224-8100) and flexible online class scheduling with multiple start dates throughout the year. Their focus on serving underserved populations and providing education that directly satisfies government-mandated homebuyer counseling requirements sets them apart in the non-profit counseling space.

As a legitimate non-profit credit counseling organization, Credit Coalition represents a trustworthy option for consumers seeking free financial education and homebuyer preparation. The main limitation is geographic focus—they primarily serve the Houston area—and their services are educational rather than crisis-intervention oriented. Consumers needing immediate debt settlement, credit disputes, or emergency funding should look elsewhere, but those seeking foundational financial literacy and homebuyer preparation will find genuine value.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Completely free financial and homebuyer counseling and education classes
Offers classes in English and Spanish to serve diverse populations
Certificate of Completion satisfies official homebuyer education requirements for TSAHC, TDHCA, and Texas cities
Provides reverse mortgage counseling, a specialized service many non-profits don't offer
One-on-one credit counseling and financial coaching available, not just group classes
Online Zoom classes with multiple session start dates throughout the year for scheduling flexibility
Curated resources and partnerships with local programs (Habitat for Humanity, Houston Area IDA Programs, down payment assistance)
cons: Limited to Houston area and surrounding Texas cities—not a national organization
Website lacks detailed information about counselor certifications (HUD, NFCC) and specific credentials
No emergency financial assistance or lending products for consumers in crisis
Class schedule shown is sparse (only 4 upcoming events listed despite claiming regular offerings)
Website provides minimal detail about specific outcomes, success rates, or client testimonials
best_for: Low and moderate-income Houston-area residents preparing to buy a home
Consumers needing homebuyer education certificate to satisfy lender or program requirements
Spanish-speaking individuals seeking credit counseling in their preferred language
People seeking foundational financial literacy and credit knowledge without high-pressure sales
diagnosis: Credit Coalition is best for Houston-area residents seeking free, non-pressured financial education and certified homebuyer preparation, particularly those with low-to-moderate income. The main caveat is that this is an educational and counseling organization, not a lending platform—it cannot help with immediate debt crises, emergency cash needs, or credit dispute services; those requiring specialized credit repair or emergency funding should explore other category options.
services: Free "Fundamentals of Good Credit" multi-week class (online, English and Spanish)
"Life As A Homeowner" post-purchase education class
Fair housing and legal issues resources
Houston Area IDA (Individual Development Account) program connections
Down payment and closing cost assistance program referrals
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
